claim: The Claude Code task system with task create/update/list/get enables reliable multi-agent orchestration and communication through a shared task list
actionable: Use Claude Code's task system to orchestrate teams of agents; create tasks, set dependencies and blockers, and let agents communicate through task status updates.
source_title: Claude Code Task System: ANTI-HYPE Agentic Coding (Advanced)

claim: Self-validation through hooks in Claude Code agents allows them to verify their own work before handoff (e.g., validate file type, check for specific content)
actionable: Add post-execution hooks to agents that validate their work before marking tasks complete; use specialized validation scripts.
source_title: Claude Code Task System: ANTI-HYPE Agentic Coding (Advanced)

claim: Template meta-prompts teach agents to build like humans by generating consistent, validated output formats that can be reused across tasks
actionable: Create meta-prompts that generate other prompts in a specific format with embedded validation; this enables consistent agent behavior at scale.
source_title: Claude Code Task System: ANTI-HYPE Agentic Coding (Advanced)

claim: Builder and validator agent pairs provide 2x compute cost to catch errors; builders do work, validators check quality
actionable: Design multi-agent systems with specialized roles: builders focus on creating, validators focus on quality assurance.
source_title: Claude Code Task System: ANTI-HYPE Agentic Coding (Advanced)

claim: Running agents in parallel with proper task dependencies can complete in minutes what would take humans days
actionable: Structure work as parallel tasks with dependencies; let agents run concurrently and unblock as dependencies complete.
source_title: Claude Code Task System: ANTI-HYPE Agentic Coding (Advanced)

claim: Understanding core primitives (context, model, prompt, tools) is more valuable than mastering specific tools like Moltbot
actionable: Master fundamental agentic engineering principles; tools are implementation details that will change.
source_title: Claude Code Task System: ANTI-HYPE Agentic Coding (Advanced)

claim: Specialized agents should focus on one specific task extraordinarily well using a focused context window
actionable: Design agents with narrow scopes that excel at single tasks rather than generalist agents trying to handle everything.
source_title: Claude Code Task System: ANTI-HYPE Agentic Coding (Advanced)

claim: Vibe coding (using agents without understanding what they do) is risky; instead, template prompts ensure agents build consistently with human oversight
actionable: Never use agents as black boxes; build prompts that enforce specific output formats you can validate and understand.
source_title: Claude Code Task System: ANTI-HYPE Agentic Coding (Advanced)

claim: Multi-agent task systems where primary agents orchestrate sub-agents with shared task lists solve the problem of massively longer-running work
actionable: Implement primary orchestrator agents that manage task lists, with sub-agents completing specialized work and reporting back through task updates.
source_title: Claude Code Task System: ANTI-HYPE Agentic Coding (Advanced)

claim: Building the agentic layer of your codebase (meta-prompts and specialized agents) is more valuable than working on the application layer
actionable: Invest time in building reusable agent infrastructure and prompts that can generate applications, rather than building applications directly.
source_title: Claude Code Task System: ANTI-HYPE Agentic Coding (Advanced)
